hero.name: Fatih Çakır
hero.title: Backend Engineer & AI Developer
hero.subtitle: Building scalable systems at the intersection of backend, AI & DevOps.

about.heading: A bit about me
about.bio_1: I'm a Software Engineering graduate from Eastern Mediterranean University, where I studied on a full scholarship through an ABET-accredited, English-medium program (January 2026). My main focus is backend development with .NET, though I genuinely enjoy working at the point where backend meets AI and DevOps.
about.bio_2: I led the AI and Backend side of our graduation project, which won the faculty's Best Project Award. It was a Cyprus grocery price comparison platform built with RAG architecture, fuzzy search algorithms, and a smart basket optimizer. Before that I did two internships: a real-time YOLOv8 computer vision system at Bursa Çimento Fabrikası, and an inventory management system at TOFAŞ.
about.bio_3: I'm eager to step into professional life and bring fresh energy to a team that loves building great things.
about.education: BSc Software Engineering, EMU | ABET Accredited | Full Scholarship

market.title: AI Integrated Market Price Comparison
market.description: An award-winning, AI-powered system architecture built for dynamic price comparison. Engineered a .NET 8 Web API with Clean Architecture, integrated with Gemini 2.5 Flash using a RAG pipeline for natural language search, and developed a custom Smart Basket Optimization algorithm.
market.long_description: As the Team Leader & Backend/AI Developer for this award-winning platform, I architected the backend using .NET 8, ASP.NET Core Web API, and Clean (Onion) Architecture to ensure high scalability. The system utilizes Entity Framework Core and SQL Server on AWS RDS. I engineered a RAG Pipeline and AI Intent Parsing mechanism bridging the Google Gemini 2.5 Flash API with our database for real-time shopping decisions. Additionally, I developed a custom asynchronous Smart Basket Optimization algorithm to calculate the cheapest market combinations, and implemented a Fuzzy Matching Engine (Levenshtein Distance) to handle typos across massive datasets.

conveyor.title: YOLOv8 Conveyor Overflow Detection
conveyor.description: A real-time computer vision system deployed at Bursa Çimento Fabrikası to detect material overflow on conveyor belts, preventing equipment damage and safety hazards.
conveyor.long_description: Developed during my internship at Bursa Çimento Fabrikası, this system uses YOLOv8 to monitor conveyor belts in real-time via camera feeds. When overflow or anomalies are detected, the system triggers automated alerts to prevent costly equipment damage and safety incidents. This was my first hands-on experience with industrial-scale computer vision deployment.

tofas.title: TOFAŞ Spare Parts Inventory Control
tofas.description: An internal inventory management system built during my internship at TOFAŞ Türk Otomobil Fabrikası A.Ş., one of Turkey's largest automotive manufacturers, for tracking spare parts stock.
tofas.long_description: During my internship at TOFAŞ Türk Otomobil Fabrikası A.Ş., I developed an internal web application for managing spare parts inventory across the production line. The system handles full CRUD operations, stock level tracking, low-stock alerts, and reporting dashboards. Built with .NET Core and Entity Framework Core, with automated database migrations via SQLite.

wala.title: WALA — WhatsApp Like Application
wala.description: A secure real-time messaging application built with WebSockets as part of a security engineering course. Features encrypted client-server communication.
wala.long_description: Built for a security engineering course, WALA is a full-stack messaging application demonstrating secure WebSocket communication patterns. The project focused on security protocols, message integrity, authentication, and real-time bidirectional communication between multiple clients through a central server, similar to WhatsApp's architecture.

grad.role: Software Engineering Graduate — Team Lead, AI & Backend
grad.company: Eastern Mediterranean University · ABET Accredited · Best Project Award 🏆
grad.description: Graduated with full scholarship from an English-medium, ABET-accredited Software Engineering program. Led a 4-person team to win the faculty's Best Project Award with an AI-integrated market price comparison platform that used RAG architecture, fuzzy search, and smart basket optimization. EMU holds QS World Ranking recognition in the Middle East and Central Asia region.

bursa.description: Developed and deployed a real-time conveyor belt overflow detection system using YOLOv8 computer vision, reducing the risk of equipment damage on the production line.

tofas.description: Built an internal spare parts inventory management system using .NET Core and Entity Framework at TOFAŞ.

buhari.description: Worked part-time to stay productive and learn hands-on hardware skills. Diagnosed and resolved desktop and laptop hardware faults, managed component compatibility for custom PC builds, and performed clean OS installations on both Windows and Linux.

emu_start.description: Began my Software Engineering degree on a full scholarship after completing the EMU English preparatory programme.

emu_prep.description: Started at B1 level and finished the university's English preparatory programme in a single semester, then moved straight into the Software Engineering degree.
